Home › Volusia County › Port Orange › MCKENNA'S PLACE SEAFOOD SPORTS AND SPIRITS
D 60

MCKENNA'S PLACE SEAFOOD SPORTS AND SPIRITS

This grade is calculated by us from Florida DBPR public inspection records — Florida does not issue official grades. How we calculate it →

Data last updated: January 26, 2026

Loved (4.5★) but failing (D, 60/100) · safer than 5% of Volusia County

MCKENNA'S PLACE SEAFOOD SPORTS AND SPIRITS is a 4.5-star room with a D-grade kitchen — the kind of gap a star rating will never warn you about, which is the whole reason this page exists.

That D comes from 3 high-priority, 2 intermediate, and 10 basic violations cited on January 26, 2026. It currently ranks safer than 5% of Volusia County restaurants we grade — close to the bottom of the county. And the direction isn't reassuring: its recent inspections have been sliding, not recovering. If you want the flavor with fewer question marks, the safer-options list below maps higher-graded kitchens within a short drive.

Get directions Call (386) 256-2691

Safer options nearby

Higher-graded restaurants close to MCKENNA'S PLACE SEAFOOD SPORTS AND SPIRITS, by Florida DBPR grade:

  • A
    CHILI'S PORT ORANGE FL #537

    4.2★ 0.5 mi away

  • A
    ORIGINAL GIUSEPPE`S PIZZA

    4.3★ 0.7 mi away

  • A
    APPLEBEES NEIGHBORHOOD GRILL & BAR

    4★ 1.0 mi away

Inspection history

The following records are from Florida DBPR public inspection records, most recent first.

  1. January 26, 2026 · Complaint Full

    Inspection Completed - No Further Action

    3 high-priority · 2 intermediate · 10 basic

    • 01 — Food from approved source; safe, unadulterated, sound condition (×2)
    • 02 — Consumer advisory, date-marking & labeling of ready-to-eat foods
    • 06 — Potentially hazardous foods properly thawed
    • 08 — Cross-contamination: raw foods separated; food protected during prep/storage
    • 10 — In-use food dispensing utensils properly stored
    • 13 — Clean clothes; hair restraints; jewelry; fingernails
    • 14 — Food-contact & nonfood-contact surfaces properly designed/maintained (×2)
    • 22 — Food-contact surfaces clean and sanitized (×2)
    • 23 — Nonfood-contact surfaces clean
    • 35 — Insects/rodents/pests; outer openings protected against pests
    • 36 — Floors, walls, ceilings clean & maintained; proper ventilation
    • 38 — Lighting provided; fixtures shielded or bulbs protected

  2. October 24, 2025 · Routine - Food

    Inspection Completed - No Further Action

    3 high-priority · 3 intermediate · 5 basic

    • 02 — Consumer advisory, date-marking & labeling of ready-to-eat foods (×2)
    • 03 — Temperature control of foods (cold/hot holding, cooking, cooling, reheating)
    • 08 — Cross-contamination: raw foods separated; food protected during prep/storage
    • 10 — In-use food dispensing utensils properly stored
    • 14 — Food-contact & nonfood-contact surfaces properly designed/maintained
    • 23 — Nonfood-contact surfaces clean
    • 31 — Handwash sinks accessible; handwashing supplies & sign provided
    • 35 — Insects/rodents/pests; outer openings protected against pests
    • 36 — Floors, walls, ceilings clean & maintained; proper ventilation
    • 41 — Chemicals / toxic substances properly stored & used
